run_summary: EXTR is now 4 days into a sympathy-selling move after CALX's -14% print. The position is down 6.76% on thesis intact (no company-specific news, sector XLK still positive). Given the flag is still fresh (days horizon) and the mechanism — read-through sell-off in a peer — has not yet been falsified by either negative EXTR-specific news or sustained sector weakness, the thesis remains workable. Holding for mean reversion window; no new discoveries this run.

entries.name: Extreme Networks
entries.reasoning: EXTR is already open in the portfolio (4 days, down 6.76%). The discovery flag is the same underlying idea: sympathy selling off CALX. Rather than adding to a position that has not yet validated its mean-reversion assumption, prudence dictates waiting for either clear mean reversion evidence (intraday reversal, stabilization on lower volume) or falsification (EXTR-specific bad news). Track record on OVERDONE confidence-3 calls is 50% (1 hit, 1 miss), and this is a 'days' horizon setup in a noisy sector. Adding now would be over-committing to a thesis that has only 4 days of evidence. If the position reverses and thesis proves out, there will be other opportunities to size up. Skipping now preserves dry powder and avoids compounding if the move turns out to be correct.
